phrasal_verbs.meaning: if separate amounts add up to a total amount, together they form that total; combine to produce a particular result or effect
divide something such as a total amount into separate parts
increase or make something increase; gradually develop; talk about someone in a very positive way; make someone bigger, healthier and stronger
give someone money so that they do not act against you
pay money to your business partner so that you can control all of a business you previously owned together
buy large amounts of something or all of it that is available
take something that you earn or are given in one year or period of time into the next one
reach a particular number or amount
if people club together, each of them gives some money so all the money collected can be used to buy something
try to make people support you or buy something from you
give someone the same amount of money that you borrowed from them
think carefully and form an opinion about a person or a situation
make someone heavy and unable to move easily; cause problems for someone or something or make someone worried

big: make a big thing out of
long: take a long hard look at
money: put your money where your mouth is
size: that's about the size of it

idioms.meaning: a very small amount that will not have much effect
if a person or business breaks even, they neither make a profit nor lose money
not reach a particular level or to fail to achieve something you were trying to do
equal, or into two equal parts
share the cost of something with someone so that you each pay 50%

idioms.idiom: it's as broad as it's long
idioms.meaning: used for saying that you cannot choose between two things or actions because they are equal
try to be as rich, successful, etc as your neighbours
very small, because you were very young
obtain money, especially by acting dishonestly
including every part of a particular thing, situation, place, etc

idioms.idiom: six of one (and) half a dozen of the other
idioms.meaning: used for saying that two things are equally good or bad

A.title: Choose the correct word
A.instructions: Choose the correct word (A, B, C or D) to complete each sentence.

questions.sentence: The mechanic found only a ...... scratch on the bumper and said the car was fine to drive.
She completed the entire report in a ...... of the time we had expected.
Fresh fruit and vegetables were ...... at the market throughout the harvest season.
Could you please turn up the ...... on the speakers? I can barely hear the presentation.
Be careful putting that wool jumper in the washing machine — it might ...... .
The engineer measured the exact ...... of the tunnel before placing the order for materials.
After the children had played, there was a ...... of toys left in the middle of the floor.
Support for the proposed new bypass has become ...... across the region.

B.title: Correct the wrong words in bold
B.instructions: Each of the words in bold is in the wrong sentence. Write the correct words on the lines.

questions.sentence: The **uneven** monthly temperature across the city last January was just below freezing.
It is almost impossible to **expand** the full economic impact of the drought.
During the crisis, the relief agency had to **force** clean water to ensure every family received a fair share.

The **finite** plains of Central Asia stretch for thousands of kilometres without a hill in sight.
Both service stations are **vast** from the motorway junction, so the journey time to each is exactly the same.
There is a worrying **quantify** between the wages earned by men and women doing the same job.
The **imbalance** of the wind snapped the old oak tree in two during the storm.
The path through the forest was rough and **average** underfoot after the heavy overnight rains.
You can find the density of any object by dividing its **intensity** by its volume.
The wildfire spread with such **mass** that firefighters were unable to contain it for several days.

C.title: Circle the correct word or phrase
C.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

questions.sentence: After the builder admitted liability, the court awarded the family 5,000 euros in **damage / damages**.
The airline offered passengers **compensation / debit** after the four-hour delay caused them to miss connecting flights.
Your employer is legally required to **withdraw / deduct** income tax from your monthly salary before paying you.
I need to **withdraw / deduct** some cash before we head to the market — they don't accept cards.
To secure the new apartment, the agent asked for an initial **lump sum / down payment** of £400.
Many lottery winners prefer to receive their prize as a single **lump sum / down payment** rather than spread over several years.
The solicitor will **finance / deposit** the agreed funds into the client account before close of business today.
He has been **speculating / depositing** on emerging market currencies for the past month and has done rather well.
Setting up a direct **debt / debit** is the easiest way to make sure your utility bills are always paid on time.
The start-up is still struggling to find the **sum / finance** needed to launch its second product line.

D.title: Complete using the correct form of the words
D.instructions: Write a word from the box in the correct form in each gap.

After her car repairs wiped out her entire balance, she had to arrange a/an (2) ...... with the bank.

Before accepting the job, check what employee (5) ...... you are entitled to, such as healthcare and a gym subsidy.

If you start buying (8) ......, make sure you choose companies in sectors you genuinely understand.

E.title: Write one word in each gap (phrasal verbs)
E.instructions: Write one word in each gap to complete the text.
E.passage: Planning a new business requires careful financial thinking. If running costs begin to mount (1) ...... faster than expected, the pressure can soon weigh (2) ...... on everyone involved. Sizing (3) ...... the situation — identifying every expense clearly — is the most important first step. You should then break these expenses (4) ...... into two groups: those you must pay and those you can reduce. Take the non-essential costs (5) ...... from your budget immediately, leaving only what is truly necessary. The money you save can then be used to pay (6) ...... any existing loans as quickly as possible. As your cash reserves build (7) ...... and income becomes more predictable, you can consider modest reinvestment. Always check that your projected revenue adds up (8) ...... more than your total expenses. If it does not, you may need to think about which services to cut (9) ...... entirely.

questions.sentence: weigh (2) ...... on
Sizing (3) ......
break these expenses (4) ...... into
Take the non-essential costs (5) ...... from
pay (6) ...... any existing loans
adds up (8) ...... more than
cut (9) ...... entirely

F.title: Match to make sentences
F.instructions: Drag the right-column endings (A–H) to match each numbered sentence beginning (1–8).

questions.sentence: A rival developer managed to buy **D** up the remaining plots of land before we could even make an offer.
If we club **G** together, we should be able to afford a decent leaving gift for Mr Brennan.
In ten years of long-haul routes, our head pilot has clocked **A** up more than 15,000 hours in the air.
The best way to drum **B** up enthusiasm for the launch is to offer an early-bird discount.
The three founders decided to buy **E** out their fourth partner after a serious disagreement over strategy.
I still have three days of leave remaining, so I plan to carry **C** it over to next year and use it during the spring break.
I absolutely refuse to buy **F** corrupt officials off — we will build to the proper standard or not at all.
Before signing anything, we asked our lawyer to draw **H** up a revised contract with clearer payment terms.

endings.A: up more than 15,000 hours in the air.
endings.B: up enthusiasm for the launch is to offer an early-bird discount.
endings.C: it over to next year and use it during the spring break.
endings.D: up the remaining plots of land before we could even make an offer.
endings.E: out their fourth partner after a serious disagreement over strategy.
endings.F: corrupt officials off — we will build to the proper standard or not at all.
endings.G: together, we should be able to afford a decent leaving gift for Mr Brennan.
endings.H: up a revised contract with clearer payment terms.

G.title: Phrasal verbs with together
G.instructions: Complete each sentence with the word together to practise phrasal verbs that express group action.

questions.sentence: Staff from both departments joined ...... to organise a charity auction.
It took her all afternoon to piece ...... exactly what had happened.
All the local businesses came ...... to run a joint summer festival.
He managed to scrape ...... just enough money to pay the registration fee.
The whole village pulled ...... to rebuild the community hall after the fire.
We will get through this difficult period far more easily if we all stick ......
Between us, we put ...... a fairly convincing proposal in under an hour.
After months of delays, **at slow / long** last the insurance company has agreed to pay out.
She was offered a 40 per cent **share / piece** in the new company in exchange for her initial investment.
You cannot keep asking me for loans! I'm not made **of / with** money, you know.
Affordable two-bedroom flats are a bit **thin / slim** on the ground in this part of the city.
I almost went weak **on / at** the knees when he told me what the total repair bill came to.
The committee examined each proposal **in / with** depth before reaching a final decision.
The estate agent confirmed that the rear garden is approximately 350 square metres **at / in** size.
Police warned that the suspect is still **at / in** large and urged the public not to approach him.
Nobody enjoys playing board games with him because he is such a **weak / poor** loser.
The landlord grew **fat / large** on the rental income while doing nothing to maintain the properties.
The region was once very **rich / wealthy** in silver ore, which drove its early economic growth.
This seems like a solid long-term opportunity **for / with** my money.
There was a whole **number / load** of complaints about the new parking restrictions.

K.title: Key word transformation
K.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

questions.sentence:
Certain politicians will do whatever it takes to hold on to power.
**lengths** Certain politicians will ______ to hold on to power.

I cannot understand why she is getting so upset about such a small error.
**deal** I cannot understand why she is ______ such a small error.
questions.answer: making such a big deal out of

questions.sentence:
Marco admitted he had no idea what he was supposed to be doing in the first weeks of his new role.
**depth** Marco admitted he ______ in the first weeks of his new role.

I believe the finance team is largely responsible for this budget shortfall.
**small** I hold the finance team ______ responsible for this budget shortfall.

We looked absolutely everywhere for the contract and simply could not find it.

The professor talked about the consequences of inflation for a very long time.

M.title: Word formation
M.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

questions.sentence: It is the judge's responsibility to ...... the settlement fairly among all the claimants. **PORTION**
At the ...... of summer, temperatures in the valley can reach 45°C. **HIGH**
She made a ...... of €300 from the joint account to cover the weekend's expenses. **DRAW**
The researcher attributed the ...... of the islanders to their active lifestyle and Mediterranean diet. **LONG**
A ...... of shareholders voted in favour of the new chief executive at the annual general meeting. **MAJOR**
Under ...... , the hairline fracture in the bone became clearly visible to the surgeon. **MAGNIFY**
We took out a bank loan last spring, so we now make monthly ...... of just over £280. **PAY**
The two rival towns are roughly ...... from the regional capital, so commuting times are identical. **DISTANT**
Regular moderate exercise has been shown to be highly ...... for both physical and mental wellbeing. **BENEFIT**
...... is especially important when writing business correspondence — keep your message concise. **BRIEF**

There is no point trying to ...... the process — the sooner we finish, the sooner everyone can go home. **LONG**
Each candidate was given an ...... period of twelve minutes to deliver their pitch to the panel. **LOT**

N.title: Word formation (text)
N.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.
N.passage:
Marie Curie's pioneering research into radioactivity has enormously (1) ...... our understanding of the physical world and has had a (2) ...... impact on both science and medicine. Thanks to her discoveries, we now know that atoms of certain elements are inherently unstable. To a very great (3) ......, it is Curie who is responsible for our current understanding of nuclear energy and its applications. (4) ......, without her foundational work, techniques such as radiotherapy — used today to treat millions of cancer patients — might never have been developed.

Curie did not receive immediate recognition from the scientific establishment. For years, her contributions were (5) ...... dismissed or attributed to others, in part because of deep-rooted gender prejudice within academic institutions. It was only after the confirmation of her second major discovery, polonium — an (6) ...... of her earlier research into uranium — that her reputation became firmly established worldwide.

The notion that only a tiny (7) ...... of exceptional individuals can truly grasp the principles of nuclear physics is, in large part, a myth. Those who write off Curie's work as impenetrably complex overlook the methodical clarity with which she wrote. Her laboratory notebooks are notable for their analytical (8) ......, recording every variable and measurement with extraordinary precision.

Concepts such as radioactive decay and atomic structure are admittedly (9) ...... topics to get to grips with, but they are far from impossible to understand. The reach of Curie's legacy for science, medicine and society as a whole, however, may truly be (10) ......

questions.sentence: (2) ...... (CONSIDER)

O.instructions: The prefix en-, as in enlarge, can be added to some adjectives, nouns and verbs to form new verbs. Place each word below, with en-, in the correct category.
